snapshot.name: Number of transactions in the last 28 days

signals.title: Revenue exceeds MRR by 7.8%
signals.detail: 28-day revenue ($4,919) is higher than MRR ($4,562). That usually means one-time purchases or annual upfront recognition are inflating cash above the recurring base.
signals.caveat: Compare the gap across multiple periods before making a pricing call.
signals.followup: Segment Revenue by product or store to identify the non-recurring contributor.

signals.title: New customers are 55% of active subs in 28d
signals.detail: 1,401 new customers arrived against a paid base of 2,536. Growth is healthy, but conversion to paying may still be leaving money on the table.
signals.followup: Check Conversion to Paying for the same window.

signals.title: Only 0.45 transactions per new customer in 28d
signals.detail: 627 transactions against 1,401 new customers. Most new users have not monetized yet.

signals.title: Revenue declining across 3 consecutive 28-day periods
signals.detail: Revenue: $5148 → $4984 → $4939. Sustained downtrend across 84 days. Not a single-period dip, this is structural.
signals.followup: Segment revenue by product and store to identify which stream is declining.

signals.title: MRR is flat (-0.0% over 28 days)
signals.detail: Average daily MRR moved from $4540 to $4539. New subscriptions and churn are likely cancelling each other out.
signals.followup: Pull MRR Movement chart to see if new vs churned are balancing.

signals.title: New trials up 16% vs prior 28 days
signals.detail: 281 new trials in the last 28 days vs 242 prior. Top-of-funnel is expanding.

signals.title: Churn rate improving (18.7% vs 23.7% prior)
signals.detail: 30-day average churn rate decreased from 23.7% to 18.7%. Retention is strengthening.

signals.title: Revenue per subscriber flat for 3 consecutive periods
signals.detail: ARPU (28d revenue / avg active subs): $2.03 → $1.97 → $1.95. Pricing power is not improving.
signals.followup: Segment by product duration to check if mix shift is masking ARPU movement.

contradictions.title: MRR is flat (-0.0% over 28 days)
contradictions.detail: Average daily MRR moved from $4540 to $4539. New subscriptions and churn are likely cancelling each other out.
contradictions.followup: Pull MRR Movement chart to see if new vs churned are balancing.

watchlist.title: New customers are 55% of active subs in 28d
watchlist.detail: 1,401 new customers arrived against a paid base of 2,536. Growth is healthy, but conversion to paying may still be leaving money on the table.
watchlist.followup: Check Conversion to Paying for the same window.

watchlist.title: 6 chart(s) have incomplete current-period data
watchlist.detail: Charts with incomplete recent values: Revenue, MRR, Active Subscriptions, Active Subscriptions Movement, Active Trials Movement, Churn. The latest bucket is provisional.
watchlist.caveat: RevenueCat charts always treat the current period as incomplete until the period has fully elapsed.

caveats: Charts API data is regenerated from the current snapshot of receipts. Historical values can move when refunds or chargebacks land.
The current period is always incomplete, so the newest bucket is provisional.
Revenue includes one-time purchases and non-renewing subscriptions. It is not a pure proxy for recurring health.
